title: Missing From<Utf8Error> for SieveError — manual map_err in compile()
description: compile() (lib.rs line 138-142) manually constructs SieveError inside map_err for the UTF-8 check rather than implementing From<std::str::Utf8Error> for SieveError. A From impl would let the conversion site use ? directly: from_utf8(script)?. The impl would be: impl From<Utf8Error> for SieveError { fn from(e: Utf8Error) -> Self { SieveError { message: format!("invalid UTF-8: {e}"), kind: SieveErrorKind::Utf8, source: None } } }. This is the standard Rust error-handling idiom.
